title: Consistent Low-Latency Link to On-Prem
description:
## Predictable Hybrid Bandwidth

A financial-services firm is moving part of its estate to AWS but keeps a large on-premises data center. The hybrid link must provide:

- **Consistent, predictable low latency** (their current internet-based VPN suffers from jitter that breaks a latency-sensitive trading data feed),
- **High, dedicated bandwidth** (multiple Gbps sustained),
- **Private connectivity** that does not traverse the public internet,
- Resilience so a single failure doesn't sever connectivity.

Which connectivity design best meets these requirements?

options.text: Provision **AWS Direct Connect** for the dedicated private link, and add a **Site-to-Site VPN as a backup** path for resilience if the Direct Connect link fails.
Use a **Site-to-Site VPN** alone over the public internet; it is encrypted, so latency and bandwidth will be consistent.
Deploy a **Transit Gateway** by itself; it provides the dedicated low-latency physical link to the data center.
Establish **VPC peering** between the on-premises network and the VPC for a private, high-bandwidth path.

explanation: **AWS Direct Connect** is a **dedicated, private physical connection** between the data center and AWS. Because it bypasses the public internet, it delivers **consistent low latency and predictable high bandwidth** — exactly what the jittery trading feed needs. Best practice for resilience is to pair it with a **Site-to-Site VPN backup** (or a second Direct Connect) so a single link failure doesn't cut connectivity. Option B is wrong: a VPN runs **over the public internet**, so latency and throughput are inherently **variable** — it cannot guarantee the consistency required. Option C misunderstands **Transit Gateway**: TGW is an AWS-side routing hub; it is *not* itself a link to on-prem (it would still need a Direct Connect or VPN attachment to reach the data center). Option D is invalid — **VPC peering only connects AWS VPCs to each other**, not on-premises networks.

hints.title: Internet = variable
hints.body: Anything that rides the public internet (a plain VPN) cannot promise consistent latency. Dedicated private fiber (Direct Connect) can.

hints.title: TGW and peering aren't on-prem links
hints.body: Transit Gateway routes within AWS; VPC peering joins two VPCs. Neither is a physical connection to a data center.
